assumptions: overflow: 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the subtraction operation.
The overflow flag is set to 1 if an overflow condition is detected; otherwise, it is set to 0.

checks.description: Observe that after any change to inputs A or B, the outputs result and overflow become stable (no X/Z) within an unbounded safe window.
checks.pass_condition: Both result and overflow must not be high-impedance or unknown after inputs settle; unknown values are permitted if the design legitimately leaves them undefined.
checks.trigger_condition: When inputs A or B change.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: Overflow flag must be 0 when the subtraction does not cause a sign overflow, and 1 when it does, without assuming exact timing.
checks.pass_condition: If sign bits of A, B, and result indicate overflow per spec, overflow must be 1; otherwise 0. Timing of when overflow becomes valid is not constrained.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever inputs A and B are stable.
